name: Connection Pool Exhaustion
description: Database connection pool fills up causing cascading timeouts across dependent services
root_cause: Connection pool limit reached due to slow queries or connection leaks
resolution: Increase pool size, identify slow queries, check for connection leaks, implement connection timeout

name: Disk Space Full
description: Storage volumes reach capacity causing write failures and service degradation across the cluster
root_cause: Disk space exhausted due to log accumulation, data growth, or failed cleanup jobs
resolution: Free disk space, add storage capacity, configure log rotation, clean temporary files

name: Memory Leak (OOM)
description: Progressive memory consumption leads to OutOfMemoryError and process crashes
symptoms: unable to create new native thread
root_cause: Memory leak in application code or excessive memory allocation without proper garbage collection
resolution: Increase heap size, analyze heap dumps, fix memory leaks, tune GC parameters

name: Network Partition
description: Network connectivity loss between cluster nodes causing split-brain scenarios and data unavailability
root_cause: Network switch failure, cable issues, or firewall misconfiguration isolating cluster nodes
resolution: Check physical network infrastructure, review firewall rules, verify switch health, restore connectivity

name: Cascading Service Failure
description: Single component failure triggers chain reaction of dependent service failures across the system
root_cause: Lack of fault isolation between services causing failure propagation through dependency chains
resolution: Implement circuit breakers, add bulkheads, set proper timeouts, decouple service dependencies

name: High CPU Throttling
description: CPU saturation causes processing delays, request timeouts, and degraded throughput
root_cause: Resource-intensive computation, inefficient algorithms, or insufficient CPU allocation
resolution: Optimize hot code paths, scale horizontally, increase CPU allocation, implement rate limiting

name: DNS Resolution Failure
description: DNS lookup failures prevent service discovery and inter-service communication
root_cause: DNS server overload, misconfigured DNS records, or network issues affecting DNS traffic
resolution: Check DNS server health, verify DNS records, implement DNS caching, add fallback resolvers

name: Deadlock
description: Circular resource dependency between threads or processes causing complete system stall
root_cause: Circular lock acquisition order between concurrent threads or distributed lock conflicts
resolution: Fix lock ordering, implement lock timeouts, use tryLock with backoff, reduce lock scope

name: OOM Kill
description: Operating system kills processes due to memory pressure, causing unexpected service termination
root_cause: System memory exhaustion triggering kernel OOM killer on the most memory-hungry process
resolution: Set memory limits, add swap space, optimize memory usage, implement memory monitoring alerts

name: SSL Certificate Expiry
description: Expired or invalid SSL certificates cause TLS handshake failures and broken secure connections
symptoms: unable to find valid certification path
root_cause: SSL/TLS certificate not renewed before expiration date
resolution: Renew certificates, implement automated certificate rotation, set up expiry monitoring

name: Configuration Drift
description: Inconsistent configuration across cluster nodes causing unpredictable behavior and failures
root_cause: Manual configuration changes applied inconsistently or failed configuration management sync
resolution: Use centralized config management, implement config validation, audit configuration consistency

name: Log Rotation Failure
description: Failed log rotation leads to disk space exhaustion and eventual service failure
root_cause: Log rotation daemon failed or misconfigured, logs not being cleaned up on schedule
resolution: Fix logrotate configuration, restart log rotation daemon, manually clean old logs

name: Thread Pool Saturation
description: All threads in the pool are occupied causing new requests to queue or be rejected
root_cause: Incoming request rate exceeds processing capacity or slow downstream calls hold threads
resolution: Increase thread pool size, optimize request processing, implement backpressure, add request queuing

name: Split Brain in Cluster
description: Network partition causes cluster to split into independent sub-clusters each believing they are the primary
root_cause: Network partition combined with insufficient quorum configuration allowing dual-master state
resolution: Implement proper quorum-based leader election, add fencing mechanisms, fix network partition

name: Write-Ahead Log Corruption
description: Corruption in write-ahead log prevents data recovery and causes inconsistent state
root_cause: Unexpected process termination during WAL write, disk errors, or storage subsystem failure
resolution: Restore from checkpoint, replay uncorrupted portion of WAL, check disk health

name: Block Under-Replication
description: Data blocks have fewer replicas than required, risking data loss if more nodes fail
root_cause: DataNode failures reducing available replicas below the configured replication factor
resolution: Restore failed DataNodes, trigger manual re-replication, verify replication factor settings

name: Garbage Collection Storm
description: Frequent or long GC pauses cause application freezes and timeout cascades
root_cause: Heap pressure from large object allocation or memory fragmentation triggering frequent GC cycles
resolution: Tune GC parameters, resize heap regions, reduce object allocation rate, switch GC algorithm
